message:
fix(sync): improve regex safety and add lock timeout warnings

- install.sh: Use grep -F for fixed-string matching in PATH check
  Fixes regex issues with dots in paths like ~/.local/bin
- ru: Add warning messages when lock timeout fallback is used

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -692,7 +692,8 @@ add_to_path() {
     fi
 
     # Check if already configured
-    if grep -q "export PATH=.*$dir" "$shell_config" 2>/dev/null; then
+    # Use grep -F (fixed string) to avoid regex issues with dots in paths like .local
+    if grep -qF "export PATH=\"$dir:" "$shell_config" 2>/dev/null; then
         log_info "$dir already in $shell_config"
         return 0
     fi

@@ -2806,6 +2806,8 @@ write_result() {
                 dir_lock_release "$RESULTS_LOCK_DIR"
             else
                 # Best-effort fallback: write without lock if the lock can't be acquired.
+                # Log warning to stderr (visible to user) so they know there may be data issues.
+                log_warn "Could not acquire results lock after 30s, writing without lock (may cause data issues)"
                 printf '%s' "$line" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
             fi
         else
@@ -5078,6 +5080,8 @@ run_parallel_sync() {
                     printf '%s\n' "$result" >> "$results_file"
                     dir_lock_release "$results_lock_dir"
                 else
+                    # Best-effort fallback: warn and write without lock
+                    printf '\n⚠ Warning: Could not acquire results lock, writing without lock\n' >&2
                     printf '%s\n' "$result" >> "$results_file"
                 fi
